message:
refactor(cli): use global verbose flag for speedscore command

Removes the redundant --verbose/-v flag from the SpeedScore subcommand
and uses the global Cli verbose flag instead.

**Changes:**
- Remove verbose arg from Commands::SpeedScore struct
- Remove #[arg(short, long)] verbose from SpeedScore CLI definition
- Get verbose from ctx.is_verbose() in speedscore() function
- Remove #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)] (no longer needed)
- Update tests to check cli.verbose instead of command-level flag

**Why:**
- Consistency: Other commands use the global verbose flag
- DRY: No need to thread verbose through every subcommand
- UX: Users expect -v to work the same everywhere

Before: `rch speedscore css --verbose`
After:  `rch speedscore css -v` (or `rch -v speedscore css`)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
